title: Evidence review summaries - Canada.ca
content:
## Evidence review summaries
- Evidence Review Cycle for dietary guidance
- Phases of the Evidence Review Cycle
- Evidence review summaries
Health Canada continues to gather and assess evidence across the   three key input areas: scientific basis, Canadian context, and use of   guidance.
Summaries of the evidence underpinning dietary guidance,   published since the development of the Evidence Review Cycle (ERC),   are:
- Evidence review supporting Canada’s food guide: 2025 summary , presenting key findings from evidence reviewed between December 2018 and June 2025, in three ERC input areas.
- Food, Nutrients and Health: Interim Evidence Update 2018 ,   which focuses on the ERC scientific basis key input area providing an   overview of evidence on convincing relationships between food, nutrients   and health.
- Evidence Review for Dietary Guidance: Summary of Results and Implications for Canada’s Food Guide ,   which provides key results from the 2015 Evidence Review for Dietary   Guidance: Technical Report. This review included evidence from all three   ERC key input areas.
- Development of Canada’s food guide
- Developing an Evidence Review Cycle Model for Canadian Dietary Guidance
